title: Bicornella brevicalcar
intro: Bicornella brevicalcar, commonly known as Chinese club moss or Bicornella, is a type of plant belonging to the lycopodiophyte family, a group of ancient, non-vascular plants. Traditionally, Bicornella brevicalcar is used in Chinese medicine to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, sore throat, and inflammation, due to its anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Bicornella brevicalcar include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional Chinese medicine, Bicornella brevicalcar is often used to make herbal preparations such as decoctions and infusions, which are used to treat various health conditions.
taxonomy: Bicornella brevicalcar is a species of fungus in the family Agaricaceae. It belongs to the kingdom Fungi and the phylum Basidiomycota. Within the kingdom Fungi, Bicornella brevicalcar is classified as a member of the subkingdom Dikarya. The species further falls under the class Agaricomycetes and the order Agaricales. This classification places Bicornella brevicalcar within the fungal group characterized by the production of spores through meiosis.
